meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve stuck with basic apps (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Project Template Checklist
content.seo_description: Overcome basic app limitations with the Antigravity project template. A high-level checklist for freelancers to ship production-grade code using Claude Code.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because your boilerplate lacks architectural integrity. The Antigravity project template solves this by enforcing modular, production-ready patterns at the scaffolding phase. By integrating this with Claude Code, you automate boilerplate, ensure type safety, and achieve rapid deployment cycles that turn basic prototypes into high-value client SaaS assets.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: you’re three weeks into a client build, and the codebase has turned into a tangled web of technical debt. What started as a promising MVP has devolved into a brittle, unmaintainable mess that kills your velocity and your profit margins. You’re trapped in the 'basic app' cycle, spending more time debugging your own shaky foundations than delivering features.

It’s time to stop reinventing the wheel. The Antigravity project template isn't just a boilerplate; it's an opinionated architecture designed to scale. By pairing this framework with Claude Code, you transform from a manual coder into an architect, delegating the grunt work to AI while you maintain total control over the high-level logic and business value.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the root architecture. This ensures your project starts with proper dependency injection and modular boundaries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new enterprise-grade SaaS boilerplate using the Antigravity modular pattern with Next.js 14, Tailwind, and Prisma.

steps.title: Define Domain-Driven Entities
steps.description: Map your database schema to domain entities to ensure type safety across the entire stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate domain entities for a multi-tenant SaaS platform including User, Subscription, and Workspace models.

steps.title: Configure Automated API Contracts
steps.description: Use Antigravity's contract generator to sync frontend types with backend API routes, eliminating manual interface updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Zod schemas and TRPC routers based on the existing domain entities to ensure full-stack type safety.

steps.title: Implement Middleware Security
steps.description: Inject robust authentication and rate-limiting middleware into the Antigravity core to protect your client's production environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply secure middleware for role-based access control and request limiting to the API route handlers.

steps.title: Finalize CI/CD Pipeline Integration
steps.description: Configure automated deployment scripts that leverage Antigravity's build optimization for rapid production delivery.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and deployment to Vercel for the Antigravity structure.

pro_tips: Always use the --dry-run flag in Claude Code to audit architectural changes before applying them to your production template.
Leverage Antigravity's 'Service Layer' pattern to decouple your business logic from the HTTP request context, making your code infinitely more testable.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store your client's specific naming conventions, ensuring that every generated file matches their internal style guide.
Prioritize 'contract-first' development; if the API contract is defined, the frontend and backend can be built in parallel without integration friction.
Run periodic 'arch-lint' commands to ensure your codebase hasn't drifted from the original Antigravity structure during rapid iterations.
content.next_steps: Once your project is scaffolded, run a full suite of integration tests and deploy to a staging environment. Begin injecting your custom business logic into the service layers and iterate using Claude Code to keep the architecture clean.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your tools and start shipping professional-grade SaaS applications that command premium rates. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
